gaps: No test covers the scenario where battery level shows 0% for earbuds and charging case despite the app showing 'Connected' status on the Home screen for Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.

No test validates that battery percentage values are correctly fetched and displayed after initial connection — the existing tests only cover greyed-out/powered-off states and charge case open/closed states, not incorrect values during active connection.
No test covers battery level display accuracy over time (e.g., waiting 2-5 minutes after connection) to catch stale or incorrectly reported battery data.

No test covers the specific failure mode where battery indicators show 0% while the device is fully connected and operational — this is a distinct gap from the existing tests which focus on UI state changes (greyed out, green, yellow) rather than incorrect numerical values.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-001: Verify earbud and case battery percentages display correct values immediately after initial connection
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (both earbuds in case, case charged above 50%).
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the phone.
3. If a device is already connected, tap [Device Name] → Disconnect, then forget the device.
4. Open the charge case lid with earbuds inside.
5. Put the earbuds in pairing mode (press and hold the Bluetooth button on the charge case for 2 seconds).
6. On the phone, go to Settings → Bluetooth → tap 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' to pair and connect.
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Observe the Home screen — verify the app shows 'Connected' status for the device.
9. On the Home screen, locate the battery indicator section showing: Left earbud battery %, Right earbud battery %, and Charge case battery %.
10. Record the displayed battery percentages for all three indicators.
generated_testcases.expected: The Home screen displays 'Connected' status. The Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case battery percentages show realistic non-zero values (e.g., >0%) that reflect the actual charge state of the hardware. No indicator shows 0% unless the respective component is truly depleted.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-002: Verify battery percentages remain accurate 5 minutes after connection (stale data detection)
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (both earbuds >50%, case >50%).
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap [Device Name] → connect if not already connected.
3. Verify Home screen shows 'Connected' status.
4. Record the Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case battery percentages shown on the Home screen.
5. Do NOT navigate away from the app. Leave the app open and visible.
6. Wait 2 minutes. Observe if the battery percentages update or remain static.
7. Wait an additional 3 minutes (total 5 minutes). Observe if the battery percentages update or remain static.
8. After 5 minutes, tap the Home screen refresh/pull-down gesture (if available) or navigate to Device → Audio Settings and back to Home to force a refresh.
9. Record the battery percentages again.
generated_testcases.expected: Battery percentages remain accurate and non-zero throughout the 5-minute observation period. If the app auto-refreshes, values should remain consistent with actual hardware state. After manual refresh, values should still be non-zero and realistic. No indicator shows 0% while the device is connected and operational.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-003: Verify battery percentages after disconnect and reconnect cycle (no 0% false reading)
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (>50%).
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → verify Home screen shows 'Connected' and battery percentages are non-zero.
3. Record the current battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
4. Place earbuds in the charge case and close the lid.
5. Wait 10 seconds. Observe the Home screen — verify status changes to 'Disconnected' or device disappears.
6. Open the charge case lid.
7. Wait up to 30 seconds for reconnection.
8. Verify the Home screen shows 'Connected' status again.
9. Immediately observe the battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
10. Record the displayed values.
generated_testcases.expected: After reconnection, the Home screen shows 'Connected' status. Battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case display realistic non-zero values. No indicator shows 0% immediately after reconnection.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-004: Verify battery percentages after app force-kill and relaunch (no 0% false reading)
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (>50%) and connected to the phone.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → verify Home screen shows 'Connected' and battery percentages are non-zero.
3. Record the current battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
4. Fully close (force-kill) the Poly Studio Mobile app from the phone's recent apps menu (swipe away).
5. Wait 10 seconds.
6. Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
7. Observe the Home screen — verify 'Connected' status appears.
8. Immediately observe the battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
9. Record the displayed values.
generated_testcases.expected: After app force-kill and relaunch, the Home screen shows 'Connected' status. Battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case display realistic non-zero values. No indicator shows 0% due to stale cached data or failed re-fetch on app restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-005: Verify battery percentages after phone reboot (no 0% false reading)
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (>50%) and connected to the phone.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → verify Home screen shows 'Connected' and battery percentages are non-zero.
3. Record the current battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
4. Reboot the phone.
5. After phone restarts, ensure Bluetooth is enabled.
6. Wait for automatic reconnection (up to 60 seconds).
7. Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
8. Observe the Home screen — verify 'Connected' status appears.
9. Immediately observe the battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
10. Record the displayed values.
generated_testcases.expected: After phone reboot and reconnection, the Home screen shows 'Connected' status. Battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case display realistic non-zero values. No indicator shows 0% due to stale cached data or failed re-fetch after phone restart.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-006: Verify battery percentages when one earbud is at low battery and the other is charged
steps: 1. Prepare Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC with one earbud at low battery (<10%) and the other earbud charged (>50%). Charge case should be >50%.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → ensure device is connected (Home screen shows 'Connected').
3. Observe the battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
4. Record the displayed values.
5. Tap the battery indicator area (if tappable) to see detailed view.
6. Observe if the low-battery earbud shows the correct low percentage (not 0%) and the charged earbud shows a higher percentage.
7. Navigate to Device → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → Environment, then navigate back to Home.
8. Observe battery percentages again.
generated_testcases.expected: The low-battery earbud shows its actual low percentage (e.g., 5-10%), not 0%. The charged earbud shows its actual higher percentage. The charge case shows its actual percentage. Values persist correctly after navigating away and back. No indicator incorrectly shows 0%.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-007: Verify battery percentages do not show 0% when charge case is open and earbuds are in use
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (>50%) and connected.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → verify Home screen shows 'Connected'.
3. Record battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
4. Remove both earbuds from the charge case and wear them.
5. Leave the charge case lid OPEN.
6. Observe the Home screen battery indicators — verify all three percentages are still displayed and non-zero.
7. Wait 1 minute. Observe if any battery percentage drops to 0%.
8. Place earbuds back in the charge case and close the lid.
9. Open the lid again and observe battery percentages.
generated_testcases.expected: With earbuds removed and case lid open, all three battery indicators (Left earbud, Right earbud, Charge case) continue to display realistic non-zero values. No indicator shows 0% while the device is connected and operational.

generated_testcases.title: TC-BAT-008: Verify battery percentages after firmware update (no 0% false reading post-update)
steps: 1. Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are charged (>50%) and connected.
2. Open Poly Studio Mobile app → verify Home screen shows 'Connected' and battery percentages are non-zero.
3. Record the current battery percentages.
4. Navigate to Device → Firmware Update.
5. If an update is available, proceed with the firmware update. If no update is available, note this and proceed to step 6.
6. After firmware update completes (or skip if no update), navigate back to Home screen.
7. Observe the Home screen — verify 'Connected' status appears.
8. Observe the battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case.
9. Record the displayed values.
generated_testcases.expected: After firmware update (or checking for update), the Home screen shows 'Connected' status. Battery percentages for Left earbud, Right earbud, and Charge case display realistic non-zero values. No indicator shows 0% due to post-update state mismatch.
